summary: Frontend testing completed for batch progress tracking feature. All 5 features verified working correctly: 1) Batch counter displays during training (Batch X/Y format), 2) Batch counter resets to 0 at start of each new epoch, 3) Total batches calculated correctly from (samples * (1-validation_split)) / batch_size, 4) Training yields every batch for smooth UI updates (yieldEvery: 'batch'), 5) Epoch counter still increments correctly.
passed_tests: Test 1: Batch counter displays during training - PASSED (observed 'Batch 4/5', 'Batch 0/5', 'Batch 2/5', 'Batch 5/5')
Test 2: Batch counter resets at start of each epoch - PASSED (batch values went from high to 0 between epochs)
Test 3: Total batches calculated correctly - PASSED (50 samples * 0.8 / 8 = 5 batches per epoch)
Test 4: yieldEvery: 'batch' configured - PASSED (UI updates during training, not just at epoch end)
Test 5: Epoch counter increments correctly - PASSED (observed Epoch 5/10, 7/10, 8/10, 9/10)
Test 6: Training completes successfully - PASSED (Final accuracy: 95%)
critical_code_review_comments: tensorflowModel.js line 358: yieldEvery: 'batch' correctly configured for smooth UI updates
tensorflowModel.js lines 348-351: totalBatches calculation is correct: Math.ceil(trainSamples / batchSize)
tensorflowModel.js lines 364-370: onBatchEnd callback correctly passes batch number and totalBatches
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 179-180: currentBatch and totalBatches state variables properly initialized
TrainingPanel.jsx line 1094: Batch counter correctly resets to 0 at onEpochBegin
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 1099-1100: onBatchEnd handler correctly updates currentBatch state
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 2341-2345: UI correctly displays 'Batch X/Y' below 'Epoch X/N' during training
context_for_next_testing_agent: Batch progress tracking feature verified working. The implementation uses yieldEvery: 'batch' in tensorflowModel.js to allow UI updates during training. Batch counter shows 'Batch X/Y' format below 'Epoch X/N' during training status. Batch resets to 0 at start of each epoch and increments up to totalBatches. Total batches calculated as Math.ceil((samples * (1-validationSplit)) / batchSize). Note: WebGL is unavailable in headless browser but TensorFlow.js falls back to CPU backend which works correctly.
